bead.title: server: reconcile loop kills fresh server-managed workers ~5s after bead claim (spawn/kill churn every tick)
bead.description:
# PROBLEM
Observed live on eitri 2026-07-05 20:40-21:00 EDT (ddx v0.6.2-alpha104-4162-g772c3f55a): for every project with ready beads (ddx, snorri, pqueue), the supervisor spawns a fresh `ddx work --server-managed` worker on its 10s tick; the worker claims the top ready bead, prints 'readiness check: starting', then ~5s later receives SIGINT ('Cancel received, shutting down gracefully', exit status 130) and dies. The next tick spawns a replacement, which repeats the cycle. No bead ever executes; each cycle can invoke the codex-backed pre-claim intake hook, burning provider spend every 10 seconds per project. The cayce worker — whose queue was empty and which therefore never claimed — survived the whole window, so the kill correlates with the claim path, not worker age. Worker record example: /Users/erik/Projects/snorri/.ddx/workers/worker-20260706T004055-5645 (state=failed, last_error='exit status 130', log ends 'readiness check: starting' → 'Cancel received').

This is a distinct manifestation from ddx-c8506158 (duplicate long-lived workers on sindri after handle loss): here the workers hold live in-memory handles (spawned by the current server process) and are killed, not duplicated.

# ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS
Something in the per-tick reconcile path revokes the fresh worker's claim or stops it by ID. Grep entry points:
- cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:253-260 ReconcileAt: every tick, staleIDs (running-on-disk, no in-memory handle) go through stopStaleDiskEntry.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:1406-1459 stopStaleDiskEntry: unclaims the bead recorded on the stale record (`store.Unclaim(beadID)`) — with ~150 historical worker dirs per project, a stale record naming the SAME top-ready bead the live worker just claimed would release the live worker's claim out from under it; the worker's claim-guard then cancels the attempt (matches the observed SIGINT-equivalent graceful cancel). Verify whether stopStaleDiskEntry can fire repeatedly (writeRecord failure leaves state=running) or whether many distinct stale records reference the current top bead.
- cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:286-344 snapshotWorkers: confirm hasWorkerHandle is true for subprocess-launched workers of the CURRENT server generation; if false they land in staleIDs and get stop-stale'd (claim released + record flipped) within one tick of spawning — which matches the observed ≤10s lifetime exactly.

# PROPOSED FIX
Determined by which hypothesis verifies. Candidates: make snapshotWorkers classify current-generation subprocess workers via OS-liveness (kill(pid,0)) instead of in-memory handle only; make stopStaleDiskEntry skip unclaim when the bead's current claim does not belong to the stale record's worker/attempt; one-shot stale sweep at startup instead of per-tick.

# NON-SCOPE
- ddx-c8506158 (adopt-or-terminate handle-less duplicates across restarts) — sibling, same root-cause area but different invariant.
- workerprobe TLS registration failure (ddx-a6e99ad3).
- Operator-attention relaunch suppression not being honored (separate bead).

Related: ddx-c8506158, ddx-a6e99ad3.

No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. New test TestSupervisorReconcile_FreshClaimingWorkerSurvivesTicks in cli/internal/server/ proves: a subprocess-launched worker of the current manager generation that has claimed a bead survives >=3 consecutive ReconcileAt ticks (not stopped, claim intact).
2. New test TestStopStaleDiskEntry_DoesNotUnclaimForeignClaim proves: stopStaleDiskEntry on a stale record whose bead is currently claimed by a DIFFERENT live worker/attempt does not release that claim.
3. Live verify on eitri documented in closing note: with desired_count=1 restored for ddx/snorri/pqueue, workers survive >10 minutes (pgrep count stable across ticks) and at least one bead reaches a terminal outcome (success/no_changes/etc), no exit-130 churn in .ddx/workers records.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'Supervisor|StaleDisk' -count=1 green
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes
